SOFI.title: What's Going On With SoFi Technologies Stock Thursday?

macro_events.event_summary: Investor concern is growing about private credit's heavy exposure to software/tech borrowers, yet inflows continue into ETFs with significant software and private-credit-adjacent holdings.
primary_drivers: Private credit lenders have outsized exposure to leveraged software buyouts, raising credit-quality concerns
Retail/ETF flows are masking underlying credit risk buildup
Potential mark-to-market and default risk for BDCs and asset managers tied to software LBO loans

beneficiaries.rationale: Large bank with regulated balance sheet positioned to gain share if private credit faces losses/outflows; has publicly flagged private credit risks and could reclaim leveraged loan origination from non-bank lenders.

Moody's benefits from increased demand for private credit ratings and risk analytics as investors scrutinize portfolio credit quality; higher volatility in credit markets typically boosts ratings-revision and research revenue.

losers.rationale: Ares Capital is the largest publicly-traded BDC with substantial exposure to software/tech middle-market LBO loans; direct write-down risk if software borrowers face refinancing stress.
Blackstone Secured Lending has heavy software and tech sector concentration in its direct lending portfolio; marks would decline if software LBO credit deteriorates.
Ares Management is one of the largest private credit asset managers; fee-related earnings and fundraising pace tied directly to private credit's perceived health and software-loan quality.
Blackstone's credit segment (including BCRED) is a dominant direct lender to software LBOs; sentiment hit on private credit weighs on AUM growth expectations.
Blue Owl's flagship BDC has material exposure to software and recurring-revenue loans; NAV and dividend coverage at risk if software borrower defaults rise.
Blue Owl Capital is heavily weighted to direct lending (GP stakes + credit); perceived deterioration in private credit underwriting hits valuation multiple.
FS KKR Capital BDC has meaningful software/tech middle-market exposure; trades at sensitivity to private credit sentiment and potential NAV markdowns.
Main Street Capital — BDC peer with correlated trading behavior; even lower software exposure gets swept up in sector de-rating on private credit headlines.

headlines: Investors are worried about private credit’s close ties to software stocks. So, why is money flooding into these ETFs?
